commands.review_summary:
The branch extends the DeepSeek DSML text filter to capture complete DSML invoke blocks, parse them into synthetic tool calls, discard truncated captures, and skip DSML recovery when native tool calls already exist.

PR surface: Source +110, Tests +94. Total +204 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main filters DeepSeek DSML text but only creates executable completion tool calls from native choiceDelta.tool_calls, while the linked issue supplies a live Foundry DeepSeek V4 payload showing DSML surfaced as assistant text. I did not run a live provider repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor action remains because the PR needs real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the security-sensitive text-to-tool recovery boundary, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(agents): recover tool calls from DeepSeek DSML text markup
target.body:
## Summary

Fix two bugs in the DeepSeek DSML tool-call recovery path that caused incorrect behavior:

**Fix 1 — Discard truncated DSML blocks**: When a stream ends abruptly (no close token like `</｜DSML｜tool_calls>`), the captured DSML body is incomplete garbage. Previously `flush()` stored these truncated blocks for recovery. Now it discards them.

**Fix 2 — Skip recovery when native tool_calls exist**: When the model uses both the structured tool_calls API and also emits DSML planning text, recovery would inject duplicate tool calls. Now recovery is skipped entirely when the response already contains native `toolCall` blocks.

## Changed files

- `src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.ts` — Truncated stream discard in `flush()` path
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` — Native tool_calls guard before DSML injection
- `src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.test.ts` — Added truncated-stream discard test

## Verification

```
Terminal: pnpm test src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.test.ts

 ✓ |unit-fast| src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.test.ts (19 tests) 3ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  19 passed (19)
```

Behavioral checks:
- Complete DSML block with close token → captured and parsed for recovery
- Truncated stream without close token → captured body discarded, no ghost tool calls
- Native tool_calls present on response → DSML recovery skipped entirely, no duplicate execution
- No native tool_calls + complete DSML → recovery injects tool calls and sets stopReason to toolUse

Closes #85918
